alignments.candidate_evidence: SQL proficient (joins, GROUP BY, subqueries/CTEs, window functions; queries BigQuery directly); Python basic, actively learning (data-analysis/pandas track)
alignments.job_requirement: Bring at least basic knowledge or hands-on experience in SQL and/or Python

alignments.candidate_evidence: Designs, specs and directs LLM coding agents to build production systems; owns evaluation methodology, benchmarking and calibration; actively learning Python for data analysis
alignments.job_requirement: Have a passion for technology, data and AI and the impact it can have on the world

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; designs AI agent specifications and evaluation methodologies to solve business problems
alignments.job_requirement: Are able to think analytically and creatively, especially in applying technology solutions to core business problems

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google career demonstrates stakeholder management at scale; directs engineering partners on LLM agent feature requests; experience with transformation and change management
alignments.job_requirement: Are able to build relationships with senior management and influence decision-makers; confident to present to C-level executives

alignments.candidate_evidence: Fluent English (professional); no Dutch language evidence provided
alignments.job_requirement: Are fluent in Dutch and English

gaps.gap: No Dutch language fluency; English-only proficiency against stated requirement of 'fluent in Dutch and English'
gaps.mitigation: Candidate could pursue accelerated Dutch language training (6–12 months) if seriously interested; Celonis may consider conditional offer with language-learning clause for onboarding in Madrid

gaps.gap: Role is junior graduate/early-career program (L3 equivalent); candidate is L4–L5 seniority with eight years' experience and two promotions at Google
gaps.mitigation: Significant overqualification limits career fit and retention risk; candidate would enter a structured mentorship program designed for recent graduates, not experienced senior ICs

gaps.gap: Value Engineering is not a target role family; pure customer engineering with sales partnering lacks the strategy/transformation/business operations scope that defines target families
gaps.mitigation: If role required deep process mining or strategic process automation consulting, fit would be stronger; as described, it is a customer delivery and sales-support function

gaps.gap: No demonstrated process mining, Celonis platform, or enterprise SaaS deep-dive experience
gaps.mitigation: Onboarding program includes training; candidate's SQL and Python basics are entry points, but 12-month ramp typical for graduates

output.level_rationale: Explicit graduate/internship-level program (Orbit, 12-month cohort structure) with stated bachelor/master degree and work/internship experience typical for L3 entry roles. No management scope or senior-IC ownership required.
output.summary: Associate Applied AI Engineer is an explicit L3 graduate program (Orbit) in customer Value Engineering—a sales-support and customer delivery function, not a target Strategy & Operations, Product Operations, or Business Transformation family. Candidate is L4–L5 experienced with eight years and two Google promotions, causing significant overqualification and career-fit misalignment. Critical gap: Dutch language fluency is stated as a requirement; candidate has only English. Technical (SQL, Python basics, AI agent design) and analytical skills are present, but seniority mismatch, non-target role family, and language requirement make this a poor strategic fit.
uncertainties: Company size/stage context not stated; this is noted but does not affect core recommendation since role is explicitly junior graduate program
Candidate's German native language could support relocation to Germany/Munich HQ, but Madrid onboarding and Benelux client base center on Dutch
Extent of candidate's interest in customer delivery and AI prototyping vs. strategy/transformation unclear from profile
